title: camissoniopsis guadalupensis subsp. clementina
intro: Camissoniopsis guadalupensis subsp. clementina, also known as the Clementine Island Mariposa Tulip or Island Mariposa, is a subspecies of Camissoniopsis guadalupensis, a flowering plant in the evening primrose family. It is native to the Channel Islands of California and is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Camissoniopsis guadalupensis subsp. clementina include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and soothe skin irritations. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or topical applications, and is often used in traditional folk medicine for its various health benefits.
